title: Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
summary: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.4 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure.

title: Colombia’s Next President Taps Gómez as Incoming Finance Chief
summary: Colombian President-elect Abelardo de la Espriella named Miguel Gómez to be his finance minister, tapping the former lawmaker from a conservative political dynasty to curb a rising national debt load and restore investor confidence.

title: Asian Stocks Set to Rise After Stellar Quarter: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian equities were poised to rise Wednesday after capping their best quarter in nearly 17 years, as a rally in chipmakers and signs of US economic resilience fueled optimism about corporate earnings.

title: Nike Sees Weakness Persisting as Wall Street Patience Wears Thin
summary: Nike Inc. executives on Tuesday gave a cautious outlook and warned about elevated consumer anxiety, adding to investor concerns about the sportswear company’s painfully slow turnaround.

title: Oaktree Capital-Backed ITG Raises $312.2 Million in US IPO
summary: Digital infrastructure services firm ITG Inc. raised $312.2 million in a US initial public offering, pricing its shares below a marketed range.

title: Oil Steadies as Peace Talks Continue, Hormuz Traffic Recovers
summary: Oil steadied after the biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Shutterstock Shares Fall After Getty Says It Will End Merger
summary: Shares of Shutterstock Inc. plunged about 30% in after-hours trading after Getty Images Holdings Inc. said it would cancel a planned merger due to a ruling from UK regulators.

title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
summary: Alcoa Corp. agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in Australia, Brazil and South Africa in a deal valued at as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position among the world’s largest aluminum producers as long-term demand strengthens.

title: Nike Appoints Pfizer CFO as New Finance Chief Amid Industry Experience Questions
summary: Nina Trentmann, Senior Editor at Bloomberg News, discussed the recent appointment of Dave Denton, formerly Pfizer's CFO, as Nike's new Finance Chief. While some analysts have expressed concerns about Denton's lack of direct retail and apparel industry experience, Trentmann noted that for large companies like Nike and Pfizer, deep industry knowledge may be less critical than experience managing a complex, global business with a significant market capitalization and extensive supply chains. She speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Progress Software Earnings Beat Estimates, Boosts Forecast
summary: Yogesh Gupta, CEO of Progress Software, said that AI is a big driver for his company as it beat earnings estimates and boosts forecasts for the next quarter. Gupta said that his company can aggregate data for AI in a way that reduces the number of tokens necessary to run AI agents and saves customers money.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Assessing Nike vs. Adidas as World Cup Heats Up
summary: Drake MacFarlane, M Science Research Analyst, expressed cautious optimism about Nike's recent apparel sales boost, particularly from soccer jerseys, noting it as a positive short-term factor. However, he emphasized that Nike's need to strengthen its sports credentials and improve its footwear segment represents a longer-term challenge. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Market Still Regulating Despite 'Flare Ups' Says Samantha Dart
summary: Samantha Dart, co-head of global commodities research at Goldman Sachs, said that markets haven't reacted to 'flare ups' in the Strait of Hormuz because US energy exports and China imports have remained consistent, signaling the oil market is still headed in the right direction.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China’s Factory Activity Returns to Growth | The China Show 6/30/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Yao Ming on NBA Legacy, Steph Curry, Global Expansion
summary: NBA Hall of Famer Yao Ming discusses being a part of the New York–Shanghai Science & Technology Forum. He discusses the global expansion of the NBA, and the popularity of basketball around the world. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Brazil Real Becomes ‘Collateral Damage’ as Dollar Roars Back
summary: The Brazilian real recorded its worst month this year as a rebound in the dollar and shifting interest-rate expectations push investors to unwind one of their most favored carry trades.

title: Debt and Risky Bets Fuel South Korea and Taiwan's AI Stock Boom
summary: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, how an AI-driven stock boom in Taiwan and South Korea is enticing investors to take big risks on one of the world’s hottest market rallies.

title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
summary: 40年ぶり円安の先、高市首相の長期戦略、米イラン和平交渉、米経済統計、米最高裁判断

title: Citigroup CEO Brings Long-Troubled Bank Into Trump’s Graces
summary: Citigroup's CEO Jane Fraser has brought the third-largest bank back from the brink and is quickly building a rapport with President Trump and his administration, a feat that has eluded many peers. Todd Gillespie explains how she managed it  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kura Sushi to Accelerate US Growth to Fend Off Rivals’ Expansion
summary: Conveyor-belt sushi operator Kura Sushi Inc. has an ambitious target to grow its store count by 20% a year in the US, betting that it can build market share before a wave of competitors rolls in.

title: Canadian Stocks Beating US for a Second Year as Banks Surge
summary: Canada’s equity benchmark is positioned to outperform its US counterpart for a second year in a row — which would be its first back-to-back win in 15 years.

title: Trump Tariff Defeat Is Market Tailwind ‘No One Is Talking About’
summary: The sweeping tariffs underpinning President Donald Trump’s economic policy may have gone from a headwind to a tailwind for the stock market, with the Supreme Court’s decision to unwind them setting the stage for a potential earnings boon for some companies.

title: Bitcoin Slides Below $60,000 as Strategy Selloff Refuels Anxiety
summary: Bitcoin tumbled as much as 3.5% toward $58,000 on Tuesday as investors reversed an initial vote of confidence in Michael Saylor’s financing overhaul at Strategy Inc., raising fresh concerns that one of the cryptocurrency’s biggest buyers may no longer be a consistent source of demand.

title: Gold Holds Above $4,000 as Traders Eye Fed Outlook, Iran Talks
summary: Gold held above $4,000 an ounce, as traders weighed the outlook for US monetary policy following a fresh slew of economic data.

title: Breaking Down Nike's Better-Than-Expected Results
summary: Nike reported better-than-expected results last quarter, a sign that CEO Elliott Hill’s turnaround efforts are gaining some traction. Bloomberg Intelligence's Poonam Goyal has more on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: S&P 500 Climbs at End of Best Quarter Since 2020 | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chip Stocks Put Up Their Best Quarter Ever With Some Wild Swings
summary: Chip stocks posted their best quarter ever, extending an extraordinary start to the year driven by insatiable demand for artificial intelligence equipment. But after recent jitters sent the stocks tumbling, investors are wondering how much further the rally can go.

title: S&P 500 Climbs at End of Best Quarter Since 2020: Markets Wrap
summary: The best quarter in six years for stocks ended on a positive note, with chipmakers extending their surge from war-driven lows and signs of economic resilience fueling optimism about corporate earnings.

title: US Stocks Gain as S&P 500, Nasdaq Notch Best Quarter Since 2020
summary: US stocks advanced on Tuesday as the main indexes notched their best quarter in six years and traders parsed the latest economic data.

title: Rising Inflation Is Squeezing the South
summary: The "Southern squeeze" is affecting the South, where the cost of living is rising and overall wages are failing to keep pace, with median household income in the South being about $76,000, well below the national average. This is forcing residents and businesses to make tough decisions. Brett Pulley reports for us from Atlanta.  (Source: Bloomberg)
